articles.summary: US Embassy in Jordan warns citizens of Iranian attacks The US Embassy in Jordan issued an alert to its citizens amid Iranian strikes, urging them to seek shelter and remain indoors. Iran targeted several key facilities used by US forces at the al-Azraq airbase in the country.

articles.title: Ben & Jerry’s Co-Founder’s War Against Big Ice Cream
articles.summary: Ben & Jerry’s isn’t just known for ice cream, but also its support for social causes. With over $1 billion in sales last year, it’s an asset for its owner Magnum. So why has one of its founders quit and the other launched a campaign to make Ben & Jerry's independent? Bloomberg’s Akshat Rathi sits down with co-founder Ben Cohen to ask about the Free Ben & Jerry’s campaign, why businesses should pursue social causes, and the future of ice cream on a warming planet. Subscribe to Zero on Apple Podcasts, Spotify or YouTube to get new episodes every Thursday. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Gaza doctor appears on video for the first time since last February
articles.summary: Gaza doctor appears on video for the first time since last February Prominent Palestinian doctor Hussam Abu Safiya, captured by Israeli forces in Gaza in late 2024 and held in detention ever since, appeared via video link at Israel's Supreme Court on Wednesday. "Dr. Hussam is still being held in solitary confinement in Nafha prison. He appeared via screen handcuffed and shackled. The court refused to remove the shackles," lawyer Nasser Odeh told reporters outside the court. This marks the first time he was seen since a video was posted on Israeli media in late February last year.  Promine

articles.title: Iran's president says country will not 'surrender' to ceasefire violations
articles.summary: Iran's president says country will not 'surrender' to ceasefire violations Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said the country would not give in to ceasefire violations, following US attacks. "War is certainly not in the country's interest, but if they think that by violating our dignity, our territory and our homeland that we will surrender or back down, then let them only dream about it," he expressed during a speech.  Pezeshkian also referenced Gaza, saying that they were "not able to force... a small Strip to surrender after three years", adding that they will not be able to do the s

articles.title: Kuwait temporarily closes airspace due to Iranian attacks
articles.summary: Kuwait temporarily closes airspace due to Iranian attacks The Kuwait Civil Aviation Authority announced a temporary closure of its airspace as a result of continued Iranian attacks.  The country's army earlier said it was responding to "hostile" missile and drone attacks.

articles.title: Sirens sound off in Bahrain, Kuwait responds to missile and drone attacks
articles.summary: Sirens sound off in Bahrain, Kuwait responds to missile and drone attacks Air raid sirens have activated in Bahrain once again, with the Ministry of Interior instructing people to "remain calm and head to the nearest safe place". Elsewhere in Kuwait, the army said it was responding to "hostile" missile and drone attacks.

articles.title: Iran's Aerospace Force chief vows 'hell' in the region after US strikes
articles.summary: Iran's Aerospace Force chief vows 'hell' in the region after US strikes Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Aerospace Force chief  has vowed "hell" in the region following the latest wave of US attacks. "Are you making the sacred Strait of Hormuz unsafe?! We will make the region hell for you from all over Iran," Majid Mousavi was reported as saying according to the semi-official news agency Tasnim.  "This is the response to the audacity of the Americans in the region, God willing," he added.

articles.title: Fresh explosions heard nearby Tehran
articles.summary: Fresh explosions heard nearby Tehran Explosions have been reported in the areas of Pishva and Qarchak near the capital Tehran, with the source of the blast originating from outside the city, Iran's Mehr news agency reported.

articles.title: Thai Long Bonds Draw Funds With Steepest Curve in Emerging Asia
articles.summary: Thailand’s yield curve has shifted to the steepest in emerging Asia, with some investors saying that makes its longer-dated bonds look attractive given a divergence in interest-rate expectations there versus regional peers.

articles.title: US army says latest attacks on Iran 'completed'
articles.summary: US army says latest attacks on Iran 'completed' The US military said that it "completed" its latest wave of strikes against Iran, where it targeted "Iranian military surveillance capabilities, communication systems, and air defense sites" across the country.  "U.S. Marine Corps, Air Force, and Navy assets fired precision munitions on Iranian targets that posed a threat to U.S. forces and international commercial ships transiting regional waters," the US Central Command stated on X.

articles.summary: India is taking unprecedented measures to restore confidence in its national student examination system after a series of scandals fueled angry protests from young people and put pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government.

articles.title: Iran will deliver 'crushing and decisive' blows to US aggression
articles.summary: Iran will deliver 'crushing and decisive' blows to US aggression Iran’s top joint military command said that its armed forces were ready to deliver a "crushing and decisive" response to any US aggression.

articles.title: Iran says it launched strikes on 18 US targets in Kuwait, Bahrain
articles.summary: Iran says it launched strikes on 18 US targets in Kuwait, Bahrain Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it launched attacks on 18 American targets located in Kuwait and Bahrain.  The strikes targeted the Ali Al Salem and Ahmed Al Jaber US bases in Kuwait, as well as Sheikh Isa Air Base in Bahrain, according to the IRGC.

articles.title: New blasts heard across Iran
articles.summary: New blasts heard across Iran Fresh explosions were reported in various cities across Iran, including Sirik in the south, Karaj in the west, and Abyek in the Qazvin province.

articles.title: Sirens sound off in Bahrain, as Iran announces targeting US fleet
articles.summary: Sirens sound off in Bahrain, as Iran announces targeting US fleet Iranian state media is reporting that the army is targeting the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain through drones. It added that the attack is in retaliation to Washington's assaults on southern Iran.  Meanwhile, sirens are sounding off in Bahrain.

articles.title: Tehran denies Trump claims that Iranian officials contacted him
articles.summary: Tehran denies Trump claims that Iranian officials contacted him Tehran's state media denied US President Donald Trump's claims that he had direct contact with Iranian officials following American attacks on the country.  "Trump's false claim that Iranian officials contacted him is a cover to evade war with Iran," an unnamed official was quoted as saying.  Trump on Wednesday told Fox News that he spoke directly with Iranian officials who asked him to stop strikes on the country.

articles.title: Activist Elliott Hits Back at Gold Miner Northern Star Over Sale
articles.summary: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management LP has hit back at Australia’s biggest gold stock Northern Star Resources Ltd. by urging the beleaguered miner’s board to take urgent action and reconsider a sale as its valuation flounders.

articles.title: Iran warns it will attack any vessels transiting Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: Iran warns it will attack any vessels transiting Hormuz Strait Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, the country's top joint military command, said it will target any ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Tasnim news agency It also added that the strait is now "completely closed to all types of vessel", following fresh US attacks against the country. The statement comes after Iran struck two vessels attempting to pass through the strategic waterway. articles.title: Trump says US will strike Iran 'very hard' if no nuclear deal is reached
articles.summary: Trump says US will strike Iran 'very hard' if no nuclear deal is reached US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that Washington will resume bombing Iran "very hard" if no nuclear deal is reached. "We're going to be attacking them and attacking them very hard," Trump told reporters, citing Iran's downing of an American Apache helicopter. He also revealed for the first time that the US has been seizing millions of barrels of Iranian oil nightly. articles.summary: Opinion: As World Cup host, the US is sportsmaxxing through war and genocide As the 2026 Fifa  World Cup  kicks off in North America, the tournament is already living up to expectations of a hostile and exclusionary reception by its main host nation. With dozens of national teams from around the globe descending on the  United States  for the tournament, reports are already surfacing of harassment of players at US airports and denials of visas for federation staff members, foreign journalists and fans. articles.summary: Global equities fell to a more than one-month low as a technology-led selloff deepened and the war in Iran showed little signs of ending. Oil rose.

articles.title: Iranian media reports explosions in south near Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iranian media reports explosions in south near Strait of Hormuz Iranian media early Thursday reported explosions in the country's south near the Strait of Hormuz after Washington acknowledged carrying out new strikes. Explosions were heard in the southern port city of Bandar Abbas, the island of Qeshm, the cities of Minab and Sirik, they said. Iranian sources reported hits by "enemy projectiles" in Qesham and the cities of Kargan and Sirik.

articles.title: Oil Climbs as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Threaten Fragile Truce
articles.summary: Oil rose after a second day of US military strikes on Iran, with the Islamic Republic announcing a halt to all vessels through the Strait of Hormuz in response, putting further strain on a fragile ceasefire.

articles.title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.

articles.title: ‘Negotiate with bombs’: Hegseth defends second night of US strikes on Iran
articles.summary: US Central Command has described the attacks as a result of 'Iran’s unwarranted and continued aggression'.

articles.title: US military says carrying out strikes against Iran
articles.summary: US military says carrying out strikes against Iran US forces began launching additional strikes against multiple targets in Iran at 5:15 pm (21:15 GMT), US Central Command said in a post on X on Wednesday. "The strikes are in response to Iran’s unwarranted and continued aggression," the military said. Iran's state broadcaster IRIB reported that an explosion was heard in the southern port city of Bandar Abbas.

articles.title: Pentagon chief says strikes on Iran will be 'strong' and 'clear'
articles.summary: Pentagon chief says strikes on Iran will be 'strong' and 'clear' US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said on Wednesday that upcoming strikes against Iran will be "strong" and "clear", suggesting the military action could stretch into a second night. "Those strikes that'll happen tonight will be strong; they will be clear. If they happen to happen tomorrow night, they will be strong and they will be clear," Hegseth told journalists at US Central Command headquarters in Tampa, Florida.

articles.title: Trump directs interim US intelligence chief Bill Pulte to downsize agency
articles.summary: Interim ODNI chief Bill Pulte has been slammed by Democrats as a Donald Trump loyalist with no intelligence background.
